article.para: Tags are used to refer to images. A tag forms a part of the image's name. Unlike labels, tags can be freely defined, and they are usually used to indicate a version number.
If a tag exists in multiple images, the newest image is used. The image maintainer decides which tags to assign to the container image.
The conventional tag format is repository name: image version specification (usually version number). For example, the tag for the latest published image of &slea; 15 SP2 would be suse/sle15:15.2.

important.title: The latest tag now points to &suselinux; 16
important.para: Since 15 January 2026, the latest tag for the core-infrastructure &bcia;s (bci-base, bci-init, bci-minimal, bci-micro, bci-busybox) points to &suselinux; 16, not to &slsa; 15. Pulling registry.suse.com/bci/bci-base:latest today therefore gives you a 16.0-based image.

If you want to stay on &slsa; 15, always pin to an explicit version tag (for example 15.7) in your FROM lines, in your CI pipelines and in any podman pull command. &slsa; 15 SP7 &bcia;s remain maintained until the end of general support for SP7 (31 July 2031). For SP6 and earlier, refer to .

See
and
for the original announcement.
